title: Практики исключений: что бросать и ловить

cards.prompt: TryParse is the Try* pattern: it doesn't throw, it returns a Boolean and an out parameter; an overflowing value yields false and 0 (default), printing ok=False v=0
cards.code: using System; bool ok = int.TryParse("999999999999999", out int v); Console.WriteLine($"ok={ok} v={v}");

cards.prompt: Restore state: the withdrawal (100->50) is rolled back when the deposit throws, then rethrown; there are no side effects, printing rolledback from=100 to=0
cards.code: using System; int from=100, to=0; string outcome; void Withdraw(){ from-=50; } void Deposit(){ throw new InvalidOperationException("deposit failed"); } try { Withdraw(); try { Deposit(); } catch { from+=50; throw; } outcome="committed"; } catch (Exception) { outcome="rolledback"; } Console.WriteLine($"{outcome} from={from} to={to}");

cards.prompt: The static throw helper ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull is called instead of constructing and throwing; it throws ArgumentNullException with ParamName equal to the argument name arg
cards.code: using System; string r; void M(object arg){ ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(arg); } try { M(null!); r="nothrow"; } catch (ArgumentNullException e) { r = e.GetType().Name + ":" + e.ParamName; } Console.WriteLine(r);
